sections.paragraph: m. with suffix טַפִּי collect, little children, boys and girls, so called from their brisk and tripping gait (from the root טָפַף, compare עֹולֵל), Gen 34:29 43:8 45:19 46:5 opposed to young men and virgins, Eze 9:6 to men above twenty years of age, Exo 12:37. Sometimes it extends to the whole family, and is opposed only to the head of the house. 2Ch 20:13, גַּם־טַפָּם נְשֵׁיהֶם וּבְנֵיהֶם “also their families, (to wit) their wives and children.” 2Ch 31:18. Gen 47:12, לְפִי הַטַּף “according to their family.” Exo 10:10 Num 32:16, 24 Num 32:24, 26 Num 32:26.
